cmd/jobs/entitlement.purpose: Cobra sub-command group for entitlement administrative jobs; exposes recalculate-balance-snapshots which drives balanceworker.Recalculator to recompute metered entitlement balance snapshots and publish results to the event bus. All dependencies come exclusively from internal.App.

patterns.name: Source all services from internal.App
patterns.description: Commands must never construct domain services locally. All dependencies (EntitlementRegistry, EventPublisher, NotificationService, Customer, Subject, Meter, Logger) are read from the package-level internal.App global.

patterns.name: Root + sub-command via cmd.AddCommand in RootCommand()
patterns.description: root.go defines RootCommand() returning a parent cobra.Command with Use='entitlement'; each job file registers itself via cmd.AddCommand(NewXxxCommand()) inside RootCommand().

patterns.name: Use cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: RunE closures must pass cmd.Context() to all domain calls, never context.Background().

key_file_guides.role: Defines the 'entitlement' parent Cobra command and registers all sub-commands via cmd.AddCommand.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New jobs must be added via cmd.AddCommand here to be reachable from the CLI.

key_file_guides.role: Canonical job implementation: constructs balanceworker.Recalculator from internal.App fields and invokes Recalculate(cmd.Context(), ...).
key_file_guides.watch_for: All RecalculatorOptions fields come from internal.App — adding a dependency requires updating cmd/jobs/internal/wire.go Application struct first and regenerating.

anti_patterns: Constructing domain services (e.g. balanceworker.Recalculator dependencies) inside the command instead of sourcing from internal.App
Adding business logic to RunE beyond orchestrating internal.App services

decisions.decision: Dependencies come exclusively from internal.App global rather than local construction.
decisions.rationale: Wire-generated initializeApplication in cmd/jobs/internal produces a fully wired Application; repeating wiring in job commands would duplicate provider graphs and risk inconsistency.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new entitlement job sub-command

cmd/jobs/internal.purpose: Single Wire DI entry point for the jobs binary: provides the Wire-generated Application struct, Viper config loading, and package-level globals (App, AppShutdown, Config) shared by all cmd/jobs sub-command packages. Never import this package from app/common — import direction is one-way outward.

patterns.name: Wire-generated initializeApplication in wire.go
patterns.description: wire.go declares the Application struct and calls wire.Build with app/common provider sets; wire_gen.go is the generated output and must never be hand-edited. After any change to wire.go or provider constructors, run 'make generate'.

patterns.name: Application struct as the exhaustive service registry
patterns.description: Every domain service needed by any sub-command must be a field on Application. Adding a dependency requires adding it as a typed field here and a corresponding common.* provider in wire.Build, then regenerating.
patterns.example: type Application struct { common.GlobalInitializer; BillingRegistry common.BillingRegistry; EntitlementRegistry *registry.Entitlement; ... }

patterns.name: Two-phase startup: loadConfig then initializeApplication
patterns.description: app.go calls loadConfig first to populate the Config global, then calls Wire-generated initializeApplication(ctx, Config). Sub-commands invoke internal.InitializeApplication from their PersistentPreRunE.

patterns.name: Package-level globals annotated with nolint:gochecknoglobals
patterns.description: App, AppShutdown, Config, ConfigFile are deliberately package-level globals — do not refactor to local variables. The nolint annotation is intentional.
patterns.example:
//nolint:gochecknoglobals
var App Application; var AppShutdown func(); var Config config.Configuration

patterns.name: metadata() bridges config to common.Metadata with service name 'jobs'
patterns.description: The metadata() function in wire.go maps config.Configuration to common.Metadata with the binary-specific service name constant 'jobs', stamping all telemetry.
patterns.example: func metadata(conf config.Configuration) common.Metadata { return common.NewMetadata(conf, version, "jobs") }

key_file_guides.role: Source of truth for which common.* provider sets are included for the jobs binary; declares Application struct fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new domain service to Application requires adding the corresponding common.* provider set in wire.Build, then running 'make generate'.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by Wire — DO NOT EDIT. Shows exact construction order and cleanup chain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never hand-edit; re-run 'make generate' after any wire.go change.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the four package-level globals consumed by all job sub-packages.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Fields on Application must exist before any sub-command can reference them at compile time.

key_file_guides.role: Public entrypoint InitializeApplication called by cmd/jobs/main.go's PersistentPreRunE.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Order matters: loadConfig runs before initializeApplication; config validation failure aborts before Wire wiring.

key_file_guides.role: Loads and validates app/config.Configuration using Viper with config.DecodeHook().
key_file_guides.watch_for: ConfigFileNotFoundError is tolerated (env-var only config); all other read errors are fatal.

anti_patterns: Editing wire_gen.go directly instead of modifying wire.go and regenerating with 'make generate'
Adding domain service construction logic inside app.go or config.go instead of wire.go provider sets
Importing cmd/jobs/internal from app/common (creates import cycle — direction is one-way: internal imports app/common, not vice versa)
Adding a new Application field without a corresponding common.* provider in wire.Build
Calling viper.SetDefault directly in internal/ instead of relying on app/config.SetViperDefaults

decisions.decision: All domain wiring is delegated to app/common provider sets rather than inline construction.
decisions.rationale: Centralizing providers in app/common means all seven binaries share identical wiring logic; changes to a constructor signature surface at Wire generation time, not at runtime.

decisions.decision: Package-level globals for App/Config rather than passing via Cobra context values.
decisions.rationale: Cobra's PersistentPreRunE runs before any sub-command RunE, making globals the simplest cross-package handoff without threading context through every layer.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new service (e.g. CurrenciesService) to the jobs Application

cmd/jobs/llmcost.purpose: Cobra sub-command group for LLM cost database operations; exposes a single 'sync' command that delegates to Wire-provided LLMCostSyncJob from internal.App — no local service construction.

patterns.name: Package-level Cmd var with init() sub-command registration
patterns.description: Cmd is declared as a package-level *cobra.Command; sub-commands are registered in init() via Cmd.AddCommand. This differs from the entitlement package's RootCommand() pattern — match the style of the file you are extending.

patterns.name: Delegate to internal.App service, never construct locally
patterns.description: RunE must call internal.App.LLMCostSyncJob.Run(cmd.Context()) directly; never construct llmcost.SyncJob or its dependencies inside the command.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the 'llm-cost' parent command and 'sync' sub-command; canonical example of the init()-based sub-command registration pattern.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LLMCostSyncJob must exist on internal.App (wired via common.LLMCost in wire.go); removing it from Application breaks this command at compile time.

anti_patterns: Constructing llmcost.SyncJob directly instead of using internal.App.LLMCostSyncJob
Adding business logic beyond delegating to the sync job

cmd/jobs/migrate.purpose: Cobra sub-command for running database migrations via the Wire-provided Migrator; validates the migration mode flag before executing and explicitly rejects the 'job' mode to prevent circular migration loops.

patterns.name: Override App.Migrator.Config at runtime before calling Migrate
patterns.description: The --mode flag value is parsed via config.AutoMigrate() and written to internal.App.Migrator.Config.AutoMigrate before calling Migrate. This is the only place in jobs that mutates App state after initialization.

patterns.name: Validate mode before execution with explicit rejection of 'job' mode
patterns.description: Uses config.AutoMigrate() to parse and validate the mode string. Explicitly rejects AutoMigrateMigrationJob mode to prevent circular use of the jobs binary for job-mode migration.
patterns.example:
if !migrationMode.Enabled() { return fmt.Errorf("migration mode is disabled") }
if migrationMode == config.AutoMigrateMigrationJob { return fmt.Errorf("migration mode cannot be job") }

key_file_guides.role: Implements the 'migrate' Cobra command; wraps common.Migrator with mode validation. Default mode is 'migration' (config.AutoMigrateMigration).
key_file_guides.watch_for: The --mode flag accepts only 'ent' and 'migration'; 'job' is explicitly rejected. Do not add migration logic (DDL, data transforms) here — those belong in tools/migrate/migrations/.

anti_patterns: Calling tools/migrate directly instead of going through internal.App.Migrator
Adding migration logic (DDL, data transforms) to this command — those belong in tools/migrate/migrations/
Allowing the 'job' migration mode through without rejection

decisions.decision: Migration mode is overridden on App.Migrator.Config rather than constructing a new Migrator.
decisions.rationale: The Wire-provided Migrator already has the correct DB client and logger; overriding just the mode field reuses all that wiring without duplication.

cmd/jobs/quickstart.purpose: Cobra sub-command group for docker-compose quickstart helpers; provides a 'cron' command that schedules billing lifecycle polling loops using gocron. Explicitly not for production — billing-worker handles these flows via Kafka/Watermill in production.

patterns.name: gocron-based polling loops for billing lifecycle
patterns.description: cronjobs.go uses gocron.NewScheduler to schedule BillingSubscriptionReconciler.All, BillingCollector.All, BillingAutoAdvancer.All at fixed intervals. Scheduler starts via s.Start() then blocks on cmd.Context().Done().

patterns.name: Nil-guard optional internal.App services before scheduling
patterns.description: ChargesAutoAdvancer may be nil when charges/credits are disabled; always check nil before scheduling its job.

patterns.name: Block on context cancellation then shutdown scheduler
patterns.description: The cron RunE blocks on <-cmd.Context().Done() and calls s.Shutdown() before returning, ensuring graceful stop of all scheduled jobs.
patterns.example: <-cmd.Context().Done(); if err := s.Shutdown(); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Composition of other job packages via init()
patterns.description: quickstart.go adds sub-commands from other job packages (e.g. billing/subscriptionsync) via Cmd.AddCommand in init(). The package acts as a meta-group aggregating related operational commands.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the 'quickstart' parent command and composes sub-commands from other job packages via init().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Explicitly documented as not for production. Do not add production-critical logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Implements 'cron' sub-command using gocron for billing lifecycle automation in quickstart environments.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Hardcodes namespace 'default' and batchSize=10 — unsuitable for multi-tenant production use. Any new cron job must nil-guard optional internal.App services before scheduling.

anti_patterns: Using this package's gocron patterns in production workers — billing-worker uses Kafka/Watermill for these flows
Not nil-checking optional internal.App services (e.g. ChargesAutoAdvancer) before scheduling
Adding production-critical billing logic to quickstart commands

decisions.decision: Quickstart cron uses gocron instead of Watermill/Kafka for simplicity.
decisions.rationale: The quickstart docker-compose environment may not have a full Kafka topology; gocron provides polling-based advancement without Kafka dependencies, making the quickstart self-contained.
